parameters.name: MLDataValidator
parameters.pitch: Internal tool for validating training datasets
parameters.spec:
Build a data validation dashboard for ML training datasets with the following core behaviors:

- DATA VALIDATION: Allow users to upload CSV files and run automated checks (schema consistency, missing values, type mismatches, range checks, category whitelists, duplicate row detection, label imbalance, data leakage heuristics, train/val/test overlap, and basic drift checks between datasets).
- CONFIGURABLE RULESETS: Let users define reusable validation rule sets per project/dataset (e.g., required columns, allowed ranges, custom Python/pandas expressions for checks where possible within the platform).
- RESULTS DASHBOARD: Show validation summaries, detailed issue tables, and downloadable reports (CSV/JSON and simple HTML/PDF-style summaries) with timestamps and dataset metadata.
- UI: Simple UI for uploading CSVs (drag-and-drop + file browser), selecting a project, choosing a validation ruleset, and viewing results.
- PERSISTENCE: Store validation configurations, run history, and detected issues in persistent storage so ML engineers can review patterns over time. Do NOT automatically use uploaded data for model fine-tuning; the app is for analysis and reporting only.
- ACCESS CONTROL: Basic authentication (e.g., admin + team accounts) so only authorized users can upload and view datasets and results.

parameters.name: MLDataValidator
parameters.pitch: Internal tool for validating training datasets

spec.description: Internal data validation dashboard for ML training datasets
features: CSV upload UI for training/validation/test datasets
Automated checks: schema consistency, missing values, type mismatches, range checks, category whitelists, duplicate row detection, label imbalance, basic drift checks, and data leakage heuristics
Configurable validation rule sets per project/dataset (required columns, allowed ranges, regex checks, etc.)
Run history with timestamps, dataset metadata, and summary statistics
Issue explorer with filters, search, and export (CSV/JSON)
Role-based access control (admin and team accounts)
non_goals: Do not override or weaken any platform or model safety controls
Do not attempt to modify how AI safety, content filters, or model guardrails operate
Do not automatically use uploaded data for training/fine-tuning; it is only for analysis and reporting
spec.persistence: Store validation configs, run metadata, and issue summaries in persistent storage for later review
spec.ui: Simple web UI with drag-and-drop + file picker for CSVs, project + ruleset selection, and results dashboard
spec.security: Internal-only app with authenticated access and logging of all validation runs

parameters.name: MLDataValidator
parameters.pitch: Internal tool for validating training datasets

spec.description: Internal data validation dashboard for ML training datasets. Used by the ML team to systematically check data quality before model training or deployment.
features: CSV upload for training/validation/test datasets

Automated checks: schema consistency, missing values, type mismatches, range checks, category whitelists, duplicate row detection, label imbalance, basic drift checks between datasets, and simple data-leakage heuristics (e.g., overlap between splits)
Configurable validation rule sets per project/dataset (required columns, allowed ranges, regex checks, simple custom expressions where supported)
Run history with timestamps, dataset metadata, and summary statistics
Issue explorer with filters, search, and export (CSV/JSON)
Role-based access control (admin and team accounts)
spec.targetAudience: Internal ML engineers, data scientists, and MLOps engineers who need a simple UI to run and review data quality checks on training datasets before they enter the ML pipeline.
spec.design: Pragmatic internal tool: single-page style layout with a left navigation (Projects, Rulesets, Runs, Settings) and a main content area. Clean, utilitarian look focused on tables and forms rather than marketing. Dark-on-light theme with clear status indicators (success/warning/error) for validation runs. Support for viewing wide tables with horizontal scrolling.
spec.typography: Sans-serif web-safe fonts (e.g., system font stack or Inter/Roboto). Emphasis on readability in tables and code-like cells (monospace for column names and rules where feasible). Use a slightly larger font for headings and section titles, with standard body size for table content.
spec.persistence: Store validation configurations, run metadata, and issue summaries in persistent storage so the ML team can track data quality trends over time. Support linking runs to project IDs or model IDs where available.
spec.ui: Simple web UI with drag-and-drop + file picker for CSVs, project + ruleset selection, and a results dashboard that shows high-level status, key metrics, and detailed issue tables.
spec.security: Internal-only app with authenticated access (admin + team accounts). Log all validation runs (who ran what, when, and on which project). No external sharing by default.
non_goals: Do not override or weaken any AI safety controls or content filters anywhere in the stack.
Do not attempt to control or modify how other AI systems handle safety guidelines.
Do not automatically use uploaded data for model training or fine-tuning; this tool is only for analysis and reporting of data quality.
